codeReview.summary: Implementation fully satisfies the feature requirements. Created a well-structured ESLint flat config (eslint.config.js) for the spikes-hosted worker with TypeScript ESLint recommended rules. Installed all required dev dependencies (eslint, typescript-eslint, @eslint/js). Fixed 2 lint errors related to unnecessary escape characters in regex patterns. All verification commands pass: lint (exit 0), typecheck (exit 0), and tests (41 pass).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The lint-worker command was already present in services.yaml, showing the mission was properly configured to anticipate this feature. The command matches exactly what was implemented: 'cd ../spikes-hosted/worker && npx eslint src test'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml line: 'lint-worker: cd ../spikes-hosted/worker && npx eslint src test'

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md already documents testing and lint procedures including the lint-worker command, demonstrating that conventions were established before implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md section 'Testing Requirements' references lint commands and validation procedures

summary: The fix-eslint-config feature is complete and correct. The ESLint flat config properly extends typescript-eslint recommended rules with sensible customizations (allowing underscore-prefixed unused variables, warning on explicit any, requiring return types on exported functions). The regex escape fixes in src/index.ts are technically correct—forward slashes don't need escaping inside character classes in JavaScript regex. All 41 tests pass, typecheck passes, and lint passes with no errors or warnings.
